headline: Making factual journalism personally rewarding | CEO & Co-Founder @ InPress
current_position_location: New York, New York, United States
current_position_description:
As the Senior Manager of Video Operations, I manage the ideation, production, and rollout of all promotional and educational video content for the DigitalOcean marketing team. Additionally, I manage the team which oversees all internal and external-facing production needs such as they relate the DigitalOcean.

Responsibilities of this role include assuring that video is an optimal and effective media, on all available mediums, for the DigitalOcean brand and its stakeholders. It is my job to see our marketing campaigns through to their successful completion, such as they relate to video. This includes building video narratives and designs, leveraging our portfolio of resources to assure the creation of quality content, recording/editing video content, and managing our video channels to maximize viewership.
latest_field_of_study: Advanced Electronic Journalism Course, Broadcast Journalism

positionList.title: Video Marketing Manager
positionList.description:
As the Video Marketing Manager, I was the first video production and marketing specialist employed by DigitalOcean. In my time as the Video Marketing Manager, built the Video Operations team such as it currently exists for DigitalOcean, and normalized a foundational level of production quality around the DigitalOcean brand which the team will continue to build off of. 

Responsibilities of this role include assuring that video is an optimal and effective media, on all mediums, for the DigitalOcean brand and it's stakeholders. It is my job to see our marketing campaigns through to their successful completion, such as they relate to video. This includes building video narratives and designs, leveraging our portfolio of resources to assure the creation of quality content, recording/editing video content, and managing our video channels to maximize viewership.

positionList.title: NATO Multimedia Journalist - Operations and Station Manager
positionList.description:
Served as the Station Manager at AFN (American Forces Network) Benelux in Mons, Belgium. This means overseeing all product quality-assurance for the radio, social media, and television departments. This role, while further removed from the production side, is a promotion and allows me to instill knowledge into newer producers while helping to guide their mission and product quality.

Prior to being the Operations Manager/Station Manager, was the lead producer and section head for the television department of AFN Benelux. This includes covering video/audio stories for the television, social media, and radio platforms within the Benelux (Belgium, Netherlands, Luxembourg) area of operation. 

Responsibilities also include timely delegation of television department producers and their products. In addition, it is my job to seek out stories and ensure quality assurance of all video products (web, tv, campaign) that are created under my supervision.

positionList.title: Military Broadcast Journalist
positionList.description: Served as a video/news producer in the television department of AFN Kunsan, while also serving as the morning show DJ in the radio section of the station. Covered multiple stories involving the life of troops on the Korean Peninsula. Awarded Best Feature Video (MILVID) in all of the military during time in Korea.

positionList.title: Senior Video Operations Manager
positionList.location: New York, New York, United States
positionList.description: Strategize, produce, and scale the video marketing mission at Isovalent across all international markets and among the entire Isovalent product portfolio.

positionList.title: Multimedia Reporter/Producer
positionList.description: My job as a Broadcast Journalist for the US Air Force is to seek out intriguing and compelling stories about service members or military affiliates and tell their stories. I am a one-man-band so to speak. It is required of me that I find the stories, make the contacts, set up the shoots, shoot the stories, conduct the interviews, produce, and disseminate all of my products in a timely manner. It is also my job to create new and interesting ways to get ours viewers engaged with our content. This may include social media campaigns, new styles of video, or ad campaigns for customers.

positionList.title: Military Broadcast Journalist
positionList.description: The primary news reporter and producer for 36th Wing Public Affairs at that time. Covered stories in multiple locations and countries while using video production experience to tell the Air Force story. Awarded Outstanding New Broadcaster for the Air Force (Pacific) during my time at this unit.

positionList.title: CEO & Co-Founder
positionList.description:
InPress is the first gamified, social news app - now with the youngest and most engaged new audience in America. We make the veggies of facts taste better than the junk food of doom scrolls.

It all started in 2015 as a journalism innovation thesis at Syracuse University, driven by a belief that democracy depends on people engaging with facts, not rage-bait. After years as an Air Force broadcast journalist and media strategist, I came back to the idea in 2023 with a mission to create the platform that news and society need most.

Today, InPress is uniting the next generation around vetted journalism, reshaping the culture of news engagement, and building a new attention economy where consuming facts is exciting, rewarding, and socially connective. With nationwide traction and partnerships with top outlets, we are proving that factual information can be more engaging and more profitable than misinformation.

InPress is not just an app. It is a movement toward a shared reality, a healthier internet, and a future where truth connects us all.

👉 Join us at www.inpress.app

positionList.title: Video Producer
positionList.description:
Was the Orientation Producer for the 2U Studios division of 2U, Inc. Responsibilities included being the first point of contact for professors as they begin the video production process with 2U, ensuring the seamless workflow of all orientation-centric productions with university partners, and creating lasting relationships with faculty that harbor successful partnerships in the future.

Additionally, oversaw all pre-and-post production needs for remote productions in multiple countries around the globe. This included scheduling, ideation, running the set, editing, and booking.

positionList.title: Digital Public Relations Manager
positionList.description:
Was the Digital Public Relations Manager (also referred to as Outreach Marketing Manager) for 2U’s Content Marketing team. In this role it was my job to oversee a team of marketing correspondents who took the journalistic, graphic, and video products that the content marketing team created, and use networking and marketing tactics to get their products seen by the largest relevant audience. Though the role included additional managerial duties, my role also involved delving into the outreach marketing mission. This means leveraging tools like Cision, Ahrefs, Majestic, and more to find the right influencers to share/post our material at the right time in the right place. The mission of the Digital Public Relations Manager is to assure that the effort that goes into creating great original journalistic content is matched by an equally great effort to get as large of an audience as possible to see it.

Upon beginning this role, it was my job to oversee all outreach marketing efforts for 2U’s nursing school partnerships. These programs included Georgetown, University of Southern California, George Washington, and Simmons College. My responsibilities eventually grew to include overseeing outreach marketing for all new university partnerships that the company signed.

positionList.title: Television Reporter (Internship)
positionList.description: As an active intern in the television department, my responsibilities included seeking, writing, and developing television stories for the daily afternoon show. In addition, I was entrusted with hosting the daily morning news update. Other tasks included helping with the engineers behind the scenes during certain shows. This internship at KUAM could not have been more immersive and was a major contributor into building the broadcaster I am today.
